wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the PolaZhenJing project overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as an AI-powered personal knowledge wiki and sharing system. Document the key value propositions, target audience, and core benefits. Describe the main features including thought management, AI integration, publishing pipeline, and social sharing capabilities. Include the technology stack summary covering FastAPI backend, React frontend, AI providers, and static site generation. Provide context about the platform's positioning in the personal knowledge management space and its unique differentiators. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include high-level architecture overview.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React 19 application structure. Document the main entry point configuration, routing setup, and component hierarchy. Explain the Vite build configuration, TypeScript setup, and development server configuration. Detail the CSS architecture, global styles, and component composition patterns. Include the application initialization process, error boundaries, and performance optimization strategies. Document the development workflow, hot module replacement, and build process. Address environment configuration, asset management, and production deployment preparation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the authentication system endpoints. Document the user registration endpoint with email/password validation and account creation flow. Detail the login endpoint including JWT token generation, expiration handling, and refresh mechanisms. Cover logout functionality and token invalidation. Document authentication middleware and protected route patterns. Include request/response schemas for all endpoints, error codes, and authentication requirements. Provide practical examples of authentication flows, token usage, and common integration patterns. Address security considerations, rate limiting, and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive application architecture documentation for PolaZhenJing backend. Explain the FastAPI application initialization, routing configuration, and middleware setup. Document the configuration management system including environment variables, database connections, and service configurations. Describe the database integration with SQLAlchemy, session management, and connection pooling. Detail the middleware stack including CORS, authentication, and custom middlewares. Explain the exception handling system and error response patterns. Include the application lifecycle, startup sequence, and shutdown procedures. Address logging configuration, health checks, and monitoring integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for PolaZhenJing. Include prerequisites and system requirements, development environment setup instructions, database configuration steps, and local deployment procedures using Docker Compose. Document the initial application setup, environment variable configuration, and first-time user onboarding process. Provide step-by-step instructions for running the application locally, accessing the dashboard, and creating the first thought. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure successful installation. Make the content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while providing sufficient technical depth.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the authentication system components. Document the login and registration page implementations, form validation, and user input handling. Explain the authentication store using Zustand, JWT token management, and user session persistence. Detail the protected route implementation, redirect logic, and authentication state synchronization. Include the API integration for authentication endpoints, error handling strategies, and loading states. Document the user experience patterns, form submission workflows, and security considerations. Address common authentication issues and troubleshooting approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thoughts API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the thoughts management system. Document CRUD operations for thought creation, reading, updating, and deletion. Cover the draft/published status management workflow and content lifecycle. Detail filtering options including status filters, tag-based queries, and date range searches. Document pagination support and sorting capabilities. Include request/response schemas for all endpoints, validation rules, and business constraints. Provide examples of thought creation workflows, bulk operations, and status transitions. Address content formatting, rich text handling, and media attachment support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed authentication system documentation for PolaZhenJing. Explain the JWT-based authentication flow, user registration and login processes, and token management. Document the authentication service implementation including password hashing, token generation, and validation. Detail the authentication schemas and request/response models. Describe the authentication dependencies and protected route patterns. Include security best practices, token refresh mechanisms, and logout procedures. Explain the user permission system and role-based access control. Address common authentication issues, debugging techniques, and security considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for PolaZhenJing's system design. Describe the high-level architecture showing the relationship between frontend React application, backend FastAPI service, database layer, and external AI providers. Document the clean architecture principles with layered separation, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Explain the design patterns used including Factory pattern for AI providers, Repository pattern for database access, and Strategy pattern for pluggable components. Include system boundaries, integration points, and the overall request/response flow. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, middleware processing, and exception handling. Provide architectural diagrams and explain the rationale behind key technical decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thought Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the thought management system. Document the dashboard component showing thought listings, filtering, and pagination. Explain the thought editor implementation with rich text editing, AI assistance integration, and draft/publish workflows. Detail the thought card component for displaying individual thoughts with status indicators and actions. Document the tag selector component for content organization and filtering. Include the custom hooks for data fetching, state management, and API integration. Explain the thought CRUD operations, real-time updates, and optimistic UI patterns. Address performance optimization for large datasets and user experience enhancements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tags API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the tag management system. Document tag creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion endpoints. Cover tag assignment to thoughts and bulk tag operations. Detail hierarchical tag organization and parent-child relationships. Document tag search and filtering capabilities including autocomplete functionality. Include request/response schemas, validation rules, and tag naming conventions. Provide examples of tag assignment workflows, tag cloud generation, and tag-based content discovery. Address tag normalization, conflict resolution, and performance optimization for large tag sets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Thought Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive thought management documentation for PolaZhenJing. Explain the thought entity model, including fields, relationships, and constraints. Document the thought service layer implementation covering CRUD operations, status management, and business logic. Detail the thought schemas for request/response validation and serialization. Describe the thought router endpoints including filtering, pagination, and search capabilities. Explain the draft/published workflow, content organization, and status transitions. Include tag assignment, category management, and content search functionality. Address performance optimization, indexing strategies, and data integrity measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend application documentation for PolaZhenJing. Explain the FastAPI application structure, initialization process, and configuration management. Document the modular architecture with separate routers for authentication, thoughts, tags, AI integration, publishing, and sharing. Describe the middleware system, exception handling patterns, and database integration. Include the application lifecycle management, startup/shutdown processes, and logging configuration. Provide an overview of the service layer architecture and how different components interact. Address security considerations, CORS configuration, and request processing pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Assistant Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the AI assistant component integration. Document the AI assistant component implementation, user interface design, and interaction patterns. Explain the integration with backend AI endpoints, real-time response handling, and streaming capabilities. Detail the conversation flow, message formatting, and user input processing. Include the component's state management, loading states, and error handling. Document the AI provider selection, request/response formatting, and content generation workflows. Address the user experience considerations, accessibility features, and performance optimization for AI interactions. Include troubleshooting common AI integration issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Integration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the AI integration system. Document content enhancement endpoints including text polishing, summarization, and creative expansion. Cover provider selection and switching mechanisms. Detail OpenAI integration including model selection, parameter configuration, and rate limiting. Document Ollama local LLM support with model management and resource allocation. Include request/response schemas for AI operations, provider-specific parameters, and fallback mechanisms. Provide examples of AI-assisted writing workflows, batch processing, and provider health monitoring. Address cost optimization, usage tracking, and error handling for AI service failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tag System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed tag system documentation for PolaZhenJing. Explain the tag entity model, hierarchical organization, and relationship with thoughts. Document the tag service implementation including tag creation, management, and lookup operations. Detail the tag schemas for validation and serialization. Describe the tag router endpoints for CRUD operations and tag-related queries. Explain tag assignment workflows, bulk operations, and tag statistics. Include tag search functionality, autocomplete features, and tag cloud generation. Address tag normalization, duplicate handling, and performance considerations for tag-heavy content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend application documentation for the React 19 TypeScript application. Document the component architecture, routing configuration, and state management using Zustand stores. Explain the page structure including dashboard, editor, login, registration, and settings components. Describe the custom hooks, API client integration, and utility functions. Include the styling approach, responsive design patterns, and component composition strategies. Document the authentication flow, protected routes, and user session management. Address performance optimization techniques, TypeScript integration patterns, and development workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Share Dialog
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the social sharing dialog component. Document the component's user interface, platform selection, and sharing workflow. Include the component's state management, modal behavior, and user interaction patterns. Document the sharing API integration, platform-specific configurations, and error handling strategies. Address the user experience design, accessibility considerations, and responsive behavior. Include troubleshooting sharing issues and platform-specific limitations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Publishing API

Describe the publishing router endpoints for triggering builds and monitoring status. Explain the MkDocs integration, theme customization, and deployment automation. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# PolaZhenJing: AI-Powered Personal Knowledge Wiki

## Project Introduction

PolaZhenJing is an innovative AI-powered personal knowledge wiki and sharing platform designed to help users capture, organize, and publish their thoughts and reflections. The project combines modern web technologies with AI capabilities to create a seamless experience for personal knowledge management and content sharing.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as a comprehensive solution for individuals who want to:
- Capture and organize their thoughts and reflections
- Leverage AI assistance for content creation and enhancement
- Publish personal knowledge as a static website
- Share insights across multiple social media platforms

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Knowledge Management**: Provide a structured way to collect and categorize personal thoughts
- **AI Integration**: Offer intelligent assistance for writing, editing, and content generation
- **Publishing Pipeline**: Automate the process of converting thoughts into publishable content
- **Social Sharing**: Enable effortless sharing across various platforms

### Target Audience
- Content creators and writers seeking AI-assisted writing tools
- Knowledge workers looking for personal knowledge management solutions
- Bloggers and thought leaders wanting to publish their ideas
- Students and researchers documenting their learning journey

## Technical Architecture

The PolaZhenJing project follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and AI services.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[React Frontend] --> B[FastAPI Backend]
    B --> C[PostgreSQL Database]
    B --> D[AI Provider Layer]
    D --> E[OpenAI API]
    D --> F[Ollama Local LLM]
    B --> G[MkDocs Static Site Generator]
    G --> H[Static Website Output]
    
    I[GitHub Pages] <---> H
    J[Social Media] <---> H
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- React 19 with TypeScript for modern web development
- Vite for fast development and build processes
- TailwindCSS for responsive styling
- Zustand for state management
- React Router for navigation

**Backend Layer**
- FastAPI for high-performance Python web framework
- SQLAlchemy with async support for database operations
- Alembic for database migrations
- Pydantic for data validation and settings management

**AI Integration Layer**
- Pluggable AI provider architecture supporting multiple backends
- OpenAI-compatible API integration
- Local Ollama LLM support
- Strategy pattern for provider selection

**Content Management**
- Thought-based content model with rich metadata
- Tagging system for content organization
- Publishing pipeline to static site generation
- Social media sharing automation

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

**Factory Pattern**: AI provider selection based on configuration
**Repository Pattern**: Database access abstraction
**Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable AI providers
**Singleton Pattern**: Cached AI provider instances

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Frontend
    participant Backend
    participant Database
    participant AIProvider
    
    User->>Frontend: Create Thought
    Frontend->>Backend: POST /thoughts
    Backend->>Database: Save Thought
    Backend->>AIProvider: Polish/Enhance Content
    AIProvider-->>Backend: Enhanced Content
    Backend-->>Frontend: Thought Created
    Frontend-->>User: Success Response
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application**
- [`backend/app/main.py`](backend/app/main.py): FastAPI application factory with middleware, exception handling, and router registration
- [`backend/app/config.py`](backend/app/config.py): Centralized configuration management using Pydantic settings
- [`backend/app/database.py`](backend/app/database.py): Async SQLAlchemy setup with connection pooling

**Frontend Application**
- [`frontend/src/App.tsx`](frontend/src/App.tsx): Root component with protected routing and authentication
- [`frontend/src/stores/authStore.ts`](frontend/src/stores/authStore.ts): Zustand store for authentication state management

### Core Modules

**Authentication System**
- [`backend/app/auth/router.py`](backend/app/auth/router.py): User registration, login, and token management endpoints
- [`backend/app/auth/service.py`](backend/app/auth/service.py): Business logic for user authentication and JWT token handling
- [`backend/app/auth/schemas.py`](backend/app/auth/schemas.py): Pydantic models for authentication requests and responses

**Thought Management**
- [`backend/app/thoughts/models.py`](backend/app/thoughts/models.py): Core content entity with status tracking and tagging
- [`backend/app/thoughts/service.py`](backend/app/thoughts/service.py): CRUD operations and business logic for thoughts
- [`backend/app/thoughts/schemas.py`](backend/app/thoughts/schemas.py): Data validation models for thought operations

**AI Integration**
- [`backend/app/ai/factory.py`](backend/app/ai/factory.py): Factory pattern implementation for AI provider selection
- [`backend/app/ai/openai_provider.py`](backend/app/ai/openai_provider.py): OpenAI-compatible API integration
- [`backend/app/ai/ollama_provider.py`](backend/app/ai/ollama_provider.py): Local LLM support via Ollama

**Content Publishing**
- [`backend/app/publish/service.py`](backend/app/publish/service.py): Orchestration for converting thoughts to Markdown and building static sites
- [`backend/app/publish/markdown_gen.py`](backend/app/publish/markdown_gen.py): Markdown generation from thought data

**Social Sharing**
- [`backend/app/sharing/service.py`](backend/app/sharing/service.py): Platform-specific sharing URL generation
- [`backend/app/sharing/router.py`](backend/app/sharing/router.py): API endpoints for sharing functionality

### Configuration Approach

**Environment-Based Configuration**
- Centralized settings management through Pydantic BaseSettings
- Support for environment variables and .env files
- Type-safe configuration with automatic validation

**Database Configuration**
- Async PostgreSQL setup with connection pooling
- Alembic migration support for schema evolution
- Automatic session management and transaction handling

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- [`backend/requirements.txt`](backend/requirements.txt): Comprehensive dependency list including FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Pydantic, and AI libraries
- Key dependencies: FastAPI 0.115.6, SQLAlchemy 2.0.36, Pydantic 2.10.3, httpx 0.28.1

**Frontend Dependencies**
- [`frontend/package.json`](frontend/package.json): Modern React stack with TypeScript, TailwindCSS, and development tools
- Key dependencies: React 19.2.4, React Router 7.14.0, Zustand 5.0.12, Vite 8.0.4

### Integration Points

**Docker Deployment**
- [`docker-compose.yml`](docker-compose.yml): Multi-service orchestration with PostgreSQL, FastAPI, and React development servers
- Volume mounting for hot-reloading during development
- Environment variable injection for production configuration

**AI Provider Integration**
- Configurable provider selection (OpenAI or Ollama)
- HTTP client abstraction for AI API communication
- Error handling and fallback mechanisms

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Thought Creation and Management**
- Rich text editor with AI assistance
- Draft, publish, and archive workflow
- Tag-based organization system
- Category and metadata management

**AI-Powered Writing Assistant**
- Content polishing and enhancement
- Automatic summarization
- Intelligent tag suggestion
- Creative expansion of ideas

**Publishing Pipeline**
- Automated Markdown generation
- Static site compilation with MkDocs
- GitHub Pages integration
- Customizable site themes

**Social Media Integration**
- Cross-platform sharing (X/Twitter, Weibo, Xiaohongshu)
- Open Graph metadata generation
- Pre-formatted share text templates
- Platform-specific URL builders

### Implementation Highlights

**Authentication Flow**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Login: User enters credentials
    Login --> Authenticated: Valid credentials
    Login --> Unauthenticated: Invalid credentials
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: View thoughts
    Authenticated --> Editor: Create/edit thought
    Authenticated --> Settings: Manage account
    Authenticated --> Unauthenticated: Logout
```

**AI Provider Selection**
- Dynamic provider switching based on configuration
- Cached provider instances for performance
- Graceful fallback handling for provider failures

**Content Publishing Process**
- Thought validation and transformation
- Markdown template rendering
- Static site generation and deployment
- Error reporting and recovery mechanisms

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[User Input] --> B[Thought Creation]
    B --> C[AI Enhancement]
    C --> D[Content Storage]
    D --> E[Publishing Pipeline]
    E --> F[Static Site Generation]
    F --> G[Deployment]
    
    H[Social Sharing] --> I[Platform URLs]
    I --> J[Cross-Platform Distribution]
    
    K[Authentication] --> L[Protected Routes]
    L --> M[User-Specific Content]
```

The PolaZhenJing project represents a sophisticated blend of modern web technologies, AI integration, and content management systems. Its modular architecture enables easy extension and customization while maintaining clean separation of concerns between frontend, backend, and AI services.

**Sources:**
- [main.py](backend/app/main.py)
- [config.py](backend/app/config.py)
- [database.py](backend/app/database.py)
- [router.py](backend/app/auth/router.py)
- [models.py](backend/app/thoughts/models.py)
- [App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx)
- [authStore.ts](frontend/src/stores/authStore.ts)
- [factory.py](backend/app/ai/factory.py)
- [openai_provider.py](backend/app/ai/openai_provider.py)
- [service.py](backend/app/publish/service.py)
- [service.py](backend/app/sharing/service.py)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)
- [requirements.txt](backend/requirements.txt)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)

</blog>
